input.headline: Why history's 'most controversial' queen was so hated

raw.headline: Why history's 'most controversial' queen was so hated

winner: Why was everyone so upset with history's "most controversial" queen? She always insisted on having a royal rumble during coronation.

candidates.text: Why did they say Queen Boudica was so hated? Because she always had a 'Caesar' salad with her wherever she went!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke uses a pun on 'Caesar salad' and the historical figure Caesar, which is somewhat clever. However, it lacks specificity about Queen Boudica's actual history and feels a bit forced. The humor is mild and not particularly surprising or original."}

candidates.text: Why did everyone dislike history's most controversial queen? Because she was always causing a royal ruckus!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat punny with 'royal ruckus,' but it's a bit generic and doesn't fully leverage the specificity of the queen being 'most controversial.' The humor is mild and not particularly surprising."}

candidates.text: Why was history's most controversial queen always booed at soccer games? Because they said she had too many "royal swaps"!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat forced and relies on a weak pun that doesn't strongly connect to the headline. It lacks specificity and surprise, making it only mildly funny."}

candidates.text: Why was everyone so upset with history's "most controversial" queen? She always insisted on having a royal rumble during coronation.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but 'royal rumble' may not be widely understood as a pun or reference, limiting its humor impact."}

candidates.text: Why was the most controversial queen so popular? Because everyone loved a good controversy queen!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 4.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat circular and doesn't fully address why the queen was hated, relying on a play on words that feels forced and lacks specificity."}
